description_long: This object is a classic CRT computer monitor with a bulky, deep chassis and a prominent, slightly curved display screen. The front features a thick, matte black bezel framing a green-tinted screen, with a control panel and ventilation grilles located below the display. The sides and rear are characterized by pronounced horizontal ventilation slats, and the overall shape is rectangular with rounded edges and a slightly protruding back. The monitor sits on two short, blocky feet that provide stability, and the casing appears to be made of hard plastic with a subtle texture. There are a few physical buttons and possibly a port area on the lower right front panel, but no visible branding or decorative elements. The design is utilitarian, typical of late 20th-century desktop monitors, and would be found in offices, schools, or urban electronic waste sites.
description: A bulky, black CRT computer monitor with a thick bezel, green screen, and horizontal ventilation slats on the sides and back. The front features a control panel and blocky feet for support.
description_view_0: Front view showing the green display, thick black bezel, and a control panel with buttons and possible ports below the screen.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the deep chassis and extensive horizontal ventilation slats, with a smooth, slightly curved back.
description_view_2: Top-down view revealing the flat, broad top surface and the monitor's overall rectangular footprint.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the ventilation slats and the monitor's depth.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, matte black plastic with squared edges and no decorative elements, providing a utilitarian frame around the display.
attribute_stand_type: The monitor is supported by two short, blocky feet integrated into the base, made of the same black plastic as the chassis, with no visible adjustability.
attribute_button_placement: Physical buttons are located on the lower right section of the front panel, grouped together below the screen.
